reasoning: The CDC's Travel Health Notice for Yellow Fever in Colombia is currently at Level 2 (Practice Enhanced Precautions) as of April 2025. Although Colombia declared a national health emergency in mid-April due to the outbreak (59 confirmed cases and 23 deaths in Tolima), Yellow fever is highly preventable through a widely available and effective vaccine. Because travelers can reliably protect themselves by getting vaccinated at least 10 days before travel, it is highly unlikely that the CDC will raise the THN to Level 3 ("Avoid Nonessential Travel") or higher for this outbreak. Level 3 is generally reserved for severe outbreaks of non-preventable diseases, massive health system collapse, or situations where travelers cannot be adequately protected. Therefore, the probability of the status exceeding Level 2 before June 1, 2025, is quite low.
